title: Ansible Inventory
learningObjectives: Create static INI and YAML inventory files
Organize hosts into groups and nested groups
Set host and group variables inline and in separate files

theory.content:
What is Inventory?

An Ansible inventory is a file (or a dynamic source) that defines the managed nodes Ansible can communicate with. It maps hosts to groups so you can run plays against subsets of your infrastructure.

INI Format

[webservers]
web1.example.com
web2.example.com ansible_user=admin

[dbservers]
db1.example.com ansible_port=2222

[datacenter:children]
webservers
dbservers

YAML Format

all:
  children:
    webservers:
      hosts:
        web1.example.com:
        web2.example.com:
          ansible_user: admin
    dbservers:
      hosts:
        db1.example.com:
          ansible_port: 2222

Host and Group Variables

Variables can be set inline (as above) or in separate files:

  • host_vars/web1.example.com.yml — per-host variables
  • group_vars/webservers.yml — per-group variables
  • group_vars/all.yml — variables for all hosts

Special Groups

  • all — all hosts in inventory
  • ungrouped — hosts not in any group

Inventory Variables

VariablePurpose
ansible_hostOverride the connection IP/hostname
ansible_userSSH username
ansible_portSSH port (default 22)
ansible_connectionConnection plugin (ssh, local, winrm)

Official Inventory Guide →
